channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Police believe family killed in Utah home was a murder-suicide
item.description: The West Valley City Police Department released more information on the family found dead inside their West Valley, Utah, home earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, NewsNation

item.title: Man accused in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing expected to appear at hearing on extradition to New York
item.description: HOLLIDAYSBURG, Pa. (AP) — The suspect charged with shooting to death a health insurance company chief executive on a Manhattan street will be taken Thursday morning to hearings on related Pennsylvania criminal charges and efforts to extradite him to New York.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The US egg industry kills 350 million chicks a year. New technology offers an alternative
item.description: WILTON, Iowa (AP) — Every year the U.S. egg industry kills about 350 million male chicks because, while the fuzzy little animals are incredibly cute, they will never lay eggs, so have little monetary value.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT McFETRIDGE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How New York prosecutors used a terrorism law in the charges against Luigi Mangione
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York prosecutors are using a 9/11-era anti-terrorism law in their case against the man charged with gunning down UnitedHealthcare's CEO outside a midtown Manhattan hotel.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rudy Giuliani ordered to appear at contempt hearing in January over failure to give up assets
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday ordered Rudy Giuliani to appear in court in early January to face claims that he should be held in contempt for failing to turn over assets including a New York apartment lease and a signed Joe DiMaggio shirt to two Georgia election poll workers who won
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kansas restaurant worker caught chopping meat on ground, owner explains why
item.description: “My wife is like, ‘What is he doing?’ and I looked behind me and I’m like, ‘He’s chopping meat!’” Greg Watkins said.
item.dc:creator: Dave D'Marko, NewsNation

item.title: Kansas restaurant worker caught chopping meat on ground, owner explains why
item.description: The video shows an employee cutting up or hammering meat on the sidewalk or drive-thru pavement area right outside Tryyaki.
item.dc:creator: Dave D'Marko, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ground squirrels found hunting and killing voles in California, new research says
item.description: The squirrels were long considered a granivorous species, but this new research suggests they may be more flexible when it comes to their diet.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, ABC 4

item.title: Horses stolen in Oklahoma City then returned, one suspect arrested
item.description: On the north side of the property, police said the fence had been cut so the suspect could go in and take the horses.
item.dc:creator: Hunter Elyse/ KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Former top aide to New York City's mayor expected in court on criminal charges, AP sources say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former top aide to New York City Mayor Eric Adams is expected in court Thursday to face corruption charges in the latest blow to an administration beset by searches, resignations and the mayor's own indictment.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Global warming can't be ignored, Montana's top court says, upholding landmark climate case
item.description: HELENA, Mont. (AP) — Montana’s Supreme Court on Wednesday upheld a landmark climate ruling that said the state was violating residents’ constitutional right to a clean environment by permitting oil, gas and coal projects without regard for global warming.
item.dc:creator: AMY BETH HANSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Some elite US universities favor wealthy students in admissions decisions, lawsuit alleges
item.description: Children of the wealthy and connected get special admissions consideration at some elite U.S. universities, according to new filings in a class-action lawsuit originally brought against 17 schools.
item.dc:creator: BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS and COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 pilots die in fiery small plane crash into vacant building near Honolulu airport
item.description: Shortly after taking off from Honolulu's Daniel K. Inouye International Airport, two pilots were killed when their small plane on a training flight crashed into a vacant building.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Social Security bill clears first hurdle in Senate
item.description: The bill, which passed with widespread support in the House, has backers lining up on both sides of the aisle.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Paris Hilton-backed youth welfare bill passes House, heads to Biden's desk
item.description: Today is a day I will never forget. After years of sharing my story and advocating on Capitol Hill, the Stop Institutional Child Abuse Act has officially passed the U.S. Congress,” Hilton said in a post on X.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Chicago White Sox stadium getting a new name for the 2025 season
item.description: The home stadium of the Chicago White Sox won't be called Guaranteed Rate Field come the start of the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KRON4

item.title: US effort to curb China's and Russia's access to advanced computer chips 'inadequate,' report finds
item.description: The U.S. Commerce Department’s efforts to curb China’s and Russia’s access to American-made advanced computer chips have been “inadequate” and will need more funding to stymie their ability to manufacture advanced weapons.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani And Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Florida pursues charge against suspect in Trump assassination attempt for a crash after his arrest
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Florida authorities announced Wednesday that they're pursuing a criminal case against a man accused of trying to assassinate President-elect Donald Trump for a car crash that occurred after law enforcement stopped traffic to catch him.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Some Walmarts testing body cameras for employees
item.description: "We are always looking at new and innovative technology used across the retail industry,” a Walmart spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin school shooter had 2 guns and got messages from man accused of plotting his own attack
item.description: Police say the shooter who killed a student and teacher at a religious school in Wisconsin this week brought two guns to the school.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Scott Bauer And Ryan J. Foley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former New Orleans priest, 93, is sentenced to life in prison for raping boy decades ago
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A judge sentenced a 93-year-old former Catholic priest Wednesday to spend the rest of his life behind bars for raping a teenage boy decades ago.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Frontier Airlines offers limited-time $299 unlimited travel pass
item.description: Frontier Airlines is offering its cheapest price on its unlimited travel pass but the introductory price expires soon
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Students assaulting educators on the rise across Texas
item.description: The number of school employees being assaulted on the job is rising in Texas. More than 2,600 assaults on educators were reported last school year, according to data from the Texas Education Agency.
item.dc:creator: Nina Banks, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico tightens bonds with immigrant community in US
item.description: The government of Mexico is strengthening bonds with its citizens in the United States, pledging to assist them at a time immigrant communities could be coming under increased scrutiny here.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: 1,000 toys dropped off for patients at Bronson Children's Hospital
item.description: The hospital will set up holiday workshops where families with patients in the hospital can shop for toys to give to their kids.
item.dc:creator: Nick Ponton, NewsNation

item.title: District attorney indicted after shooting at fugitive suspect, striking home with 3 kids inside: police
item.description: A Tennessee grand jury has indicted District Attorney Christopher Stanford on one count of reckless endangerment after he allegedly fired shots at a fugitive, striking a house.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Over 500,000 winter tires recalled due to poor traction in snow
item.description: A tire company that sells winter tires has issued a recall for more than 540,000 of its snow tires, which do not comply with North American standards due to lack of proper traction on snow.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, ABC 4

item.title: Hochul vows to haul Luigi Mangione back to New York to face terror charges in UnitedHealthcare CEO's slaying
item.description: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul demanded UnitedHealthcare CEO suspected killer Luigi Mangione be brought back from Pennsylvania to face new terrorism charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scientists find 'forever chemicals' lurking in certain smartwatch wristbands
item.description: Prior research has shown that more than a fifth of Americans wear a smartwatch or a fitness tracker — and they do so for extended periods of time.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court takes up challenge to TikTok ban
item.description: The Supreme Court announced it will take up whether a law that could ban TikTok nationwide violates the First Amendment, setting the stage for a fast-paced, high-stakes battle over free speech.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: NASA astronauts Suni Williams and Butch Wilmore, already stuck in space, are hit with new delay
item.description: Astronauts Suni Williams and Butch Wilmore, the crew of the first manned Boeing Starliner launch in early June, were initially meant to spend only about eight days in space.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: I tried the robot massage and here's how it compares
item.description: Massages aren't for everyone. But once you get one, you realize the benefits - it's relaxing and probably good for you in various ways. Now, one startup named Aescape is developing a robot to bring high-quality massages to the masses.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: GOP report recommends Liz Cheney be criminally investigated over Jan. 6 work
item.description: It’s a claim that if pursued would likely face significant roadblocks but that nonetheless comes as Trump has suggested members of the Jan. 6 panel should “go to jail” for their work.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: Indiana law shrouds executions in secrecy, prompting new pushes for public oversight
item.description: From public hangings in the town square to lethal injections witnessed by journalists, executions historically have mostly been carried out with at least some public scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Congress strikes deal to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional leaders have struck a bipartisan deal to keep the government funded beyond a looming Dec. 20 shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Things to know about the shooting at a Christian school in Wisconsin
item.description: Police in Madison, Wisconsin, are trying to determine what prompted a 15-year-old student to open fire at a private Christian school. The shooting Monday was just days before the winter break. A fellow student and a teacher were killed. The shooter also died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NBA unveils new All-Star format featuring four-team tournament
item.description: The NBA 's All-Star Game is going to be an All-Star tournament this season, with the league announcing on Tuesday that it has finalized plans to use a different format for the upcoming midseason showcase in San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4
